articles.title: Tua Tagovailoa will have an opportunity to compete for the starting job in Atlanta, GM says
articles.original_title: Tua Tagovailoa will have an opportunity to compete for the starting job in Atlanta, GM says
articles.summary: Newly signed Falcons quarterback Tua Tagovailoa will have an opportunity to compete for the starting job in Atlanta, where Michael Penix Jr. is recovering from a torn ACL he suffered in November, general manager Ian Cunningham said Friday.  ”We’re excited to have Tua, but we’re excited to have all the players that we were able to get in this free agent class.”  Cunningham said he spoke with the agent for Penix before signing Tagovailoa, who was released this week by the Miami Dolphins, and new c

articles.title: The NCAA is warning schools about travel issues ahead of March Madness. It's not the first time
articles.original_title: The NCAA is warning schools about travel issues ahead of March Madness. It's not the first time
articles.summary: Atlantic 10 commissioner Bernadette McGlade skimmed through the 12-page memo the NCAA sent out last month that highlighted potential travel issues ahead of the men's and women's basketball tournaments and couldn't help but feel a bit of deja vu.  McGlade spent time on both the men's and women's selection committees earlier in her career, including a stint as the tournament director for women's March Madness.  One that, through the years, has avoided any sort of real nightmare scenario.
